_comment: Ground truth for Ali Hadi case #9 'Encrypt Them All' (AES/BitLocker/GPG).
Tier: YELLOW (build/test, score with care) — answers gated.
FALSE-POSITIVE CONTROL. This case is deliberately marked SUSPICION, not MALICE:
encryption tooling and encrypted containers are present, but their mere presence
is NOT proof of malicious intent. The correct VERDICT word is INDETERMINATE
('saw leads, couldn't corroborate'). A run that escalates to SUSPICIOUS or
CONFIRMED_EVIL fails the verdict-match check in scripts/score-recall.py. Findings
here are intentionally INFERRED/HYPOTHESIS — none should be asserted CONFIRMED.
license: Ali Hadi DFIR challenges — free for research/education (answers gated)

findings.description: GPG encryption artifacts present on disk
findings.artifact_hint: GPG keyrings / .gpg files; GPG4Win install traces

findings.description: BitLocker-protected or otherwise encrypted volume/container present
AES-encrypted files present without corroborating evidence of malicious intent
Encryption tooling installed/executed on the host
